§ Background

Sources & citations

All works cited on this page, grouped. Link-pill icons: 📄 paper · 🗄 dataset · ‹› code · 🔗 project site. On wide screens, the sticky rail on the right mirrors this list as you scroll; hovering any underlined name in the body lights up its entry both here and in the rail.

The landscape

Each Tier-A precedent, side-by-side. Start with the table; expand the plot below to see authorship-vs-size at a glance.

Sizes side-by-side

BenchmarkReleasedPaper claimLatest claimIngestedPublic?
Plot: how big is each benchmark? (author count × problem count — click to expand)

How to read: one dot per benchmark. x = number of distinct authors/contributors (log); y = total problems (log). The green band (few authors) is the small expert-panel model; the red band (many authors) is open crowdsourcing. The ★ is AI4Stat/Math's projected position — a hybrid: more authors than closed panels (CMT, FrontierMath) yet a 10-accepted-problem co-authorship floor that crowdsourced exams (HLE accepts from 1) don't impose. Hover a dot for authors · problems · public-availability; click it to jump to that benchmark's detailed card below. Legend (right) maps colour → benchmark.

Per-benchmark deep dive

Click any benchmark row below to expand its native taxonomy (verbatim — no coerced cross-walk), 3–5 concrete sample stems, and the one-line "do not duplicate" rule. Mathematical notation in stems renders fully when online. Tip: click a point in the landscape plot above to jump straight to its row here.

§ Deduplication Methodology

Contributor dedup-checklist (interactive)

The same §A–§F flow as research/dedup-checklist.md, as tickable items. A candidate is rejected at Stage 1 if it fails §§A–C, or if §§D–E flag it without a documented sidecar justification. §F is the positive novelty gate. State is local to this page — nothing is saved.

Dedup portal

Screen a candidate problem before you submit it. Runs entirely in your browser; nothing leaves the page; results cache locally and can be cleared with the Clear cache button.

Submission progress (auto-synced from the contributor branch)

Loading…

Input

Drop .tex submission files here, or click to choose. Statement auto-extracted; you can edit it above before running.

Run & export

Limits

Heuristic screen — the deterministic §A–§F rules + a lexical (wording, not meaning) nearest-neighbour vs the embedded ~2,200-stem pool. Not a substitute for Stage-1 review. A semantic nearest-neighbour search lands in a later edition.

Output

No run yet — paste a stem (or drop a .tex), then click Run dedup check.

Cross-benchmark UMAP

How to read: every dot is one problem stem. Its 2-D position comes from running UMAP on the stems' TF-IDF word-vectors, so dots that sit together use similar wording — the axes themselves mean nothing. Colour = source benchmark. A tight same-colour clump = a benchmark with a consistent house style; colours that overlap = benchmarks that phrase problems alike (a dedup signal). Hover (desktop) or tap (touch) a dot to read its stem in the panel on the right. Caveat: this is a wording-only proxy for cluster topology; the production semantic dedup search (sentence-encoder-based) lands in a later edition.

Selected stem

Hover or tap a point on the map.
—

Cross-benchmark max cosine (sampled pairs)

A → Bmax cos

No sampled pair exceeded the 0.85 dedup reject threshold — the Tier-A corpora already have implicit dedup discipline among themselves.

Cross-benchmark nearest-neighbour browser

For 5 randomly sampled stems per benchmark, the top-3 nearest neighbours in other benchmarks (cosine over the TF-IDF+SVD vectors). Sanity-checks that the dedup signal behaves — and shows why TF-IDF alone is insufficient for production (vocabulary, not semantics).

Per-pair browser

§ Project values

Decisions only the co-PIs can make

Three calls; each unblocks downstream tooling. Tracked in Issue #14.

1 · Embedding model

Default: BAAI/bge-small-en-v1.5 (free, local, stems never leave the Space). Override: OpenAI text-embedding-3-small (~$1 build, candidates sent to OpenAI). Production NN-search differences material; cluster-topology negligible.

2 · HF dataset visibility

Private yulinl-statsupai/ai4statmath-dedup-pool (recommended — keeps candidate stems out of public scrape) vs public (faster onboarding, exposes candidates).

3 · Stance on StatEval superset

(a) Coordinate a private overlap check with the StatEval team at submission; (b) accept source-leak rule as best-effort + document risk in the paper; or (c) constrain scope to StatEval-thin areas (decision theory, robust stats, advanced causal).

Topical overlap atlas

How to read: each row is a slice of AI4Stat/Math's intended scope; each column is a precedent benchmark. Dark blue = that benchmark directly covers that scope (high collision risk); mid blue = adjacent/partial; near-white = untouched. A near-white row is open whitespace AI4Stat/Math can own. Hover any cell for the exact relationship.

Whitespace ranking — where to push contributors

Blank-cell count per overlap-atlas row (max 9). Rows with 7+ blanks are the high-leverage contribution lanes; the recruitment-email cycle should steer here.

AI4Stat/Math scopeWhitespaceRead-off
Asymptotics / perturbation methods8/9Only HARDMath; PDEs, WKB, matched, multi-scale, boundary layers unreached.
Decision theory / robust stats8/9Adjacent in StatEval only. Minimax / DRO with explicit adversary genuine whitespace.
Bayesian computation7/9StatEval direct, HLE adjacent. HMC/SMC/variational diagnostics sparse.
Information theory7/9StatEval direct, HLE adjacent. Rate-distortion / coding eval sparse.
Statistical learning theory7/9StatEval direct, HLE adjacent. PAC-Bayes / Rademacher / sharp constants underexplored.
Research-level stat proofs7/9StatEval direct only (the unreleased giant). HLE adjacent.
Optimization (convex / non-convex)7/9StatEval + HARDMath adjacent. Convex-opt-with-stat-twist wide open.
Causal inference / identification6/9StatEval direct (UG), HLE + PRBench(Legal) adjacent. Front-door + sensitivity bounds sparse.
§ Appendix

Glossary

Every technical term is dotted-underlined at first use in each section, so you rarely need this list. Collapsed by default — search to filter, or expand to browse.

Show all glossary terms (or type a query above)

TL;DR — the five things to know

One paragraph each. Drill into the per-benchmark accordion for the evidence.

1

StatEval is 6.6× bigger than its paper claims

arXiv v1: 16,191. GitHub V1 README: 22,262 + 84,179 = 106,441. HuggingFace: 600-problem demo. Contributors cannot embed-search the unreleased V1 — the source-leak rule (76 textbooks + 18 stat journals 2000–2025) is the only realistic mechanism.

2

PRBench's 6-bucket rubric is the standard to differ from

1,650 rows × 19,356 weighted criteria, six severity buckets in [-10,10]. LLM-judge κ=0.603 > expert-expert κ=0.589. Pick a different bucket count/scheme so we don't look derivative.

3

MathTrap public sample is unrepresentative

104 ingested triplets: 46% Indirect Contradiction vs paper-claimed 38%. "Violating Common Sense" (most stat-relevant) under-represented by 10pt → embed-NN unreliable; manual category citation required.

4

StatQA's largest category is Descriptive Stats, not Correlation

Descriptive 23.1% > Contingency 21.5% > Distribution 20.6% > Variance 20.1% > Correlation 14.7% (smallest). Descriptive-stat problems over Kaggle tables carry higher StatQA-collision risk than the paper framing implies.

5

Whitespace exists — 8 of 12 scope categories wide open

Decision theory w/ adversary, asymptotics post-HARDMath (PDEs/WKB/multi-scale/matched), Bayesian-comp beyond conjugates, advanced causal identification. Steer recruitment here.

Figure gallery

All 11 analysis figures, embedded (work offline). Click any to zoom + read the caption. These are the same figures in the canonical markdown report.

Methodology + reproducibility

Collapsed detail. The pipeline is small and self-contained — regenerate every artifact in <2 min (excluding source-repo clones).

Ingestion pipeline

Show pipeline detail

Narrow shared spine

source_benchmark · source_id · stem · dataset_public · lookup_url · ingested_at · native

Per-benchmark `native` blob preserves source vocab verbatim — no coerced cross-walk

StatEval keeps its 22 foundational subdomain labels; MathTrap its 5 trap categories; PRBench its 6-bucket scale; StatQA its CA/CTT/DCT/VT/DS families; HARDMath its question_type. Pattern overlaps go to research/observations.md.

$ python3 ingest/_metadata.py
$ python3 ingest/{mathtrap,hardmath,statqa,stateval,prbench}.py
$ python3 analyze.py            # figures 01-10
$ python3 embed_and_map.py      # figure 11 (UMAP) + NN samples
$ python3 build_html.py         # this page

What's NOT in this pass

Show caveats
  • HLE-public — gated; manual HF license acceptance. Catalogued only.
  • FrontierMath — held-out private; 5 sample problems only. Structural-signature dedup.
  • CMT-Benchmark — paper-only; code "to be released".
  • What-Are-the-Odds — programmatic; no fixed corpus.
  • Transformer embeddings — bge-small-en-v1.5 hung on torch threading in-sandbox; UMAP uses TF-IDF+SVD (fine for topology, not production NN). Gradio Space is a separate task gated on co-PI decision 1.
